meta.title: Session Cloning Requirements
meta.subtitle: EARS Behavioral Spec
meta.summary: Defines the behavioral requirements for `sisyphus clone "goal"` — a command that duplicates a running session with a new ID and goal. The clone is a true fork: it inherits the full session history (context, prompts, reports, snapshots, cycle history) but diverges with a new goal and trajectory. No parent-child relationship, no cross-session communication.

groups.name: CLI Command
groups.description: The `sisyphus clone` command interface and invocation behavior.
groups.context:
The clone command is run by an orchestrator (or user) from within a sisyphus session pane. It reads the current session from the environment and sends a clone request to the daemon.

  Orchestrator pane ──► `sisyphus clone "new goal"` ──► Daemon
                                                          │
                                                    ┌─────┴─────┐
                                                    ▼           ▼
                                              Duplicate       Register
                                              session dir     new session
                                                    │
                                                    ▼
                                              Spawn orchestrator
                                              (strategy mode)
                                                    │
                                                    ▼
                                              Print guidance
                                              to stdout

Command name: `clone`.

requirements.title: Clone command invocation

ears.shall: the system shall read SISYPHUS_SESSION_ID from the environment, send a clone request to the daemon with the goal and current working directory, and print structured guidance output on success

criteria.text: Command accepts a positional `task` argument (the new goal)
Command reads SISYPHUS_SESSION_ID from environment to identify the source session
Command sends a clone request to the daemon via the Unix socket
On success, prints guidance output to stdout (see Output Design group)
On failure, prints error to stderr and exits with code 1

requirements.agentNotes: Follows the same pattern as `start.ts` — positional arg, `sendRequest()`, structured output. Unlike `start`, this must run from within an existing session (SISYPHUS_SESSION_ID required).

requirements.title: Optional flags

ears.where: Where the user provides optional flags
ears.shall: the system shall accept `-c, --context <text>` for supplementary background context, `--strategy` to copy strategy.md from the source session, and `-n, --name <name>` for a human-readable session name

criteria.text: The new goal is conveyed via the positional `task` argument, NOT via --context
`-c, --context <text>` provides supplementary background context to the clone's orchestrator (same semantics as `sisyphus start --context` — background info, not the goal)
`--strategy` copies strategy.md from the source session into the clone (default: no strategy copied)
`-n, --name <name>` sets a human-readable name for the cloned session (same validation as `start --name`)
All flags are optional — the command works with just the positional goal argument

requirements.agentNotes: `--context` is supplementary background context (same as `start --context`), NOT the goal. Example: `sisyphus clone "handle auth refresh" --context "The relevant code is in src/auth/"` — the goal is auth refresh, the context points the clone to relevant files.

requirements.title: Missing session ID error

ears.if: If SISYPHUS_SESSION_ID is not set in the environment
ears.shall: then the system shall print an error indicating the clone command must be run from within a sisyphus session and exit with code 1

criteria.text: Error message clearly states that SISYPHUS_SESSION_ID is required
Error message suggests running from an orchestrator or agent pane

requirements.agentNotes: Unlike `start` which can be run from anywhere, `clone` requires a source session.

groups.name: Session Cloning Mechanics
groups.description: How the daemon creates the cloned session — what gets duplicated, what gets replaced, and what's new.
groups.context:
The clone is a TRUE DUPLICATION of the session. The clone has the same memory as the source — it knows what happened in previous cycles, what agents reported, what prompts were used. It just diverges with a new goal from the fork point.

  Source session dir              Clone session dir
  ┌────────────────┐              ┌────��───────────┐
  │ context/    ───────COPY──────►│ context/       │  (ID-replaced)
  │ prompts/    ───���───COPY──────►│ prompts/       │  (ID-replaced)
  │ reports/    ───────COPY──────►�� reports/       │  (ID-replaced)
  │ snapshots/  ───────COPY──────►│ snapshots/     │  (ID-replaced)
  │ goal.md     ───────NEW───────►│ goal.md        │  (new goal)
  │ roadmap.md  ───────RESET─────►│ roadmap.md     │  (seed template)
  │ strategy.md    (--strategy)──►│ strategy.md    │  (copy or omit)
  │ state.json  ───────FORK──────►│ state.json     │  (new ID, new task,
  │                               │                │   history preserved)
  │ logs/       ───────NEW────���──►│ logs/          │  (fresh + clone log)
  └─���──────────────┘              └────────────────┘

The key insight: the clone spawns at the next cycle number, with full awareness of everything that came before. It's a fork in trajectory, not a fresh start.

requirements.title: Context directory copying

ears.when: When the daemon processes a clone request
ears.shall: the system shall recursively copy the source session's context/ directory into the clone's session directory, preserving all files and subdirectory structure

criteria.text: All files and subdirectories in context/ are copied to the clone
File permissions and structure are preserved
The context/CLAUDE.md file is included in the copy

requirements.agentNotes: context/ is the accumulated knowledge from exploration agents, design decisions, integration maps, etc.

requirements.title: Session ID replacement in copied files

ears.when: When session files are copied to the clone
ears.shall: the system shall replace all occurrences of the source session ID with the new clone session ID in all copied text files across all duplicated directories

criteria.text: All text files in context/, prompts/, reports/, and snapshots/ have the old session ID replaced with the new one
Binary files (images, etc.) are copied without modification
Replacement is exact string match (UUID format), not regex
strategy.md (when copied via --strategy) also gets ID replacement

requirements.agentNotes: Session files contain paths and references that include the session ID. Without replacement, the clone's files would reference the source session's paths.

requirements.title: Goal file creation

ears.when: When the clone session directory is created
ears.shall: the system shall write the new goal (from the positional argument) to goal.md in the clone's session directory

criteria.text: goal.md contains the task string from the clone command
The source session's goal.md is NOT copied

requirements.agentNotes: The clone diverges with a new goal. The source goal is part of the clone's history (visible in state.json's original task), but goal.md reflects the new direction.

requirements.title: Forked state initialization

ears.when: When the clone session is registered with the daemon
ears.shall: the system shall create a state.json with a new UUID and the new task, preserving the source session's orchestratorCycles and agents arrays as history, resetting activeMs to zero

criteria.text: Clone gets a new UUID
state.json task is set to the new goal
orchestratorCycles array is preserved from source (the clone knows its history)
agents array is preserved from source (previous agents' reports are accessible)
messages array is preserved from source
activeMs resets to 0 (the clone tracks its own active time)
createdAt is set to the current time (fork point)
Status is 'active'
No parentSessionId or childSessionIds fields are written

requirements.agentNotes: The clone preserves full cycle and agent history — it knows what happened before the fork. The orchestrator spawns at cycle N+1 with awareness of all prior work. Messages are preserved as part of the session memory.

requirements.title: Session history directories copied

ears.when: When the clone session directory is created
ears.shall: the system shall copy prompts/, reports/, and snapshots/ from the source session, create a fresh logs/ directory, and write a clone event log entry

criteria.text: prompts/ is copied from source (the clone's orchestrator can see previous prompt history)
reports/ is copied from source (previous agent reports are accessible)
snapshots/ is copied from source (cycle snapshots preserved for rollback context)
logs/ is created fresh (new session, new log trail)
A clone event log entry is automatically written to logs/ noting the source session ID and fork point
roadmap.md is reset to seed template (clone builds its own roadmap for the new goal)
digest.json is NOT copied from source

requirements.agentNotes: prompts/reports/snapshots are part of the session's memory. logs/ is fresh because the clone is a new execution trail. The clone event log gives the orchestrator awareness of the fork.

requirements.title: Optional strategy copying

ears.where: Where the user provides the `--strategy` flag
ears.shall: the system shall copy strategy.md from the source session into the clone's session directory

criteria.text: With --strategy: source strategy.md is copied to clone
Without --strategy: clone gets no strategy.md (orchestrator creates its own in strategy mode)
If source has no strategy.md and --strategy is passed, no error — clone proceeds without it

requirements.agentNotes: Strategy is often session-specific enough that copying it is counterproductive. But in cases where the clone is handling a closely related sub-problem, the original strategy provides useful context.

groups.name: Clone Startup & Orientation
groups.description: How the cloned session launches and orients itself.
groups.context:
After the session directory is prepared, the daemon registers the clone and spawns an orchestrator. Because the clone has full cycle history, the orchestrator spawns at cycle N+1 in strategy mode. It sees all previous cycles and reports, plus a programmatic orientation explaining the fork.

  Daemon ──► Register clone session
                    │
                    ▼
             Create tmux session
                    │
                    ▼
             Spawn orchestrator
             (strategy mode, cycle N+1)
                    │
              ┌─────┴──────┐
              ▼            ▼
        Programmatic    --context
        orientation     (if provided)
        (always)
              │
              ���
        Orchestrator sees:
        - Full cycle history
        - All previous agent reports
        - New goal in goal.md
        - Clone event in logs/

requirements.title: Orchestrator spawned in strategy mode

ears.when: When the clone session is fully initialized
ears.shall: the system shall spawn an orchestrator in a new tmux session in strategy mode, continuing from the next cycle number after the source session's last cycle

criteria.text: Clone gets its own tmux session (same naming pattern as `start`)
Orchestrator starts in strategy mode
Clone session is tracked by the daemon (pane monitor, status dots, etc.)
Orchestrator cycle number continues from source (if source was on cycle 3, clone starts cycle 4)

requirements.agentNotes: Strategy mode is the right starting point — the clone has all the context and history but a new goal, so it needs to build a new strategy and roadmap.

requirements.title: Programmatic orientation context

ears.when: When the clone's orchestrator is spawned
ears.shall: the system shall provide programmatic context explaining that this session was cloned from another session, that it has inherited the full session history, and instructing it to build a new strategy and roadmap for its new goal

criteria.text: Context identifies this as a cloned session with a new trajectory
Context states the source session ID for reference
Context notes that context/, prompts/, reports/, and snapshots/ are inherited
Context instructs the orchestrator to reorient: build new strategy and roadmap for the new goal
Context explicitly states this session is independent — no coordination with the source

requirements.agentNotes: Delivered the same way `start --context` works. The clone's orchestrator has full history visibility through the state and file system — the orientation context makes the fork explicit.

requirements.title: User-provided context passthrough

ears.shall: the system shall pass the text to the clone's orchestrator as additional background context alongside the programmatic orientation

criteria.text: --context content is delivered alongside (not replacing) the programmatic orientation
The orchestrator sees both the programmatic orientation and the supplementary context
Without --context, only the programmatic orientation is provided

requirements.agentNotes: Supplementary context for the clone — e.g., 'Focus on the auth token refresh flow, the relevant exploration is in context/explore-auth.md'. Separate from the goal (positional arg).

groups.name: Output Design
groups.description: The guidance output printed to stdout after a successful clone — this IS the mechanism for informing the calling orchestrator.
groups.context:
Following sisyphus's 'every output is a prompt' principle, the clone command's stdout shapes the calling orchestrator's behavior. The output should make it feel confident the handoff succeeded and refocus on its own remaining scope.

  Orchestrator runs clone ──► Reads stdout
                                   │
                          ┌────────┴────────┐
                          ▼                 ▼
                    Handoff              Next steps
                    confirmation         for THIS session
                    + behavioral         │
                    guidance             ├─► Update goal
                    ('move on,           ├─► Update roadmap
                     it's handled')      └─► Update strategy

No monitor commands, no implementation details — the orchestrator should assume success and refocus.

requirements.title: Handoff confirmation and behavioral guidance

ears.shall: the system shall print a confirmation that the task was handed off to an independent session, with behavioral guidance telling the orchestrator that the work is now the clone's responsibility and it should not monitor, coordinate with, or wait for the clone

criteria.text: Output confirms the task was handed off successfully
Output states the new session ID and goal for reference
Output includes behavioral guidance: 'This task is now the other session's responsibility. You do not need to monitor it.'
Output does NOT include monitor commands (no `sisyphus status`, no `tmux attach`)
Output does NOT include details about what files were copied or how the clone was constructed
Tone is confident and reassuring — the orchestrator should feel the handoff is complete

requirements.agentNotes: The output shapes the orchestrator's emotional state — it should feel like delegating to a competent colleague, not launching a satellite that needs monitoring.

requirements.title: Next-step instructions for calling orchestrator

ears.shall: the system shall print explicit next-step instructions telling the calling orchestrator to update its own goal.md, roadmap.md, and strategy.md to remove the cloned responsibility

criteria.text: Instructs orchestrator to update goal.md to remove the scope that was cloned off
Instructs orchestrator to update roadmap.md to remove phases/steps related to the cloned scope
Instructs orchestrator to update strategy.md to adjust remaining scope
Explicitly states: do NOT coordinate with, wait for, or monitor the clone
States that the clone is fully independent and will handle its own scope

requirements.agentNotes: The most important part of the output. Without this, the orchestrator might try to coordinate with the clone or forget to update its own scope.

groups.name: Edge Cases & Error Handling
groups.description: Behavior when cloning from non-standard session states or encountering unexpected conditions.
groups.context:
The clone command may be invoked in various session states. Each needs a clear behavior.

  Source session state ──► Clone behavior
  ───��─────────────────────────────────────
  active (agents running)  ──► Allow (normal case)
  active (no agents)       ──► Allow
  paused                   ─��► Allow
  completed                ──► Reject
  not found                ──► Error

requirements.title: Clone from active session

ears.when: When a clone is requested from a session with status 'active'
ears.shall: the system shall proceed with the clone regardless of whether agents are currently running in the source session

criteria.text: Clone succeeds when source has running agents
Clone succeeds when source has no running agents
Running agents in the source session are not affected by the clone
Source session continues operating normally after clone

requirements.agentNotes: The clone reads files from the source directory — it doesn't interact with running agents.

requirements.title: Clone from paused session

ears.when: When a clone is requested from a session with status 'paused'
ears.shall: the system shall proceed with the clone normally — the source session's paused state does not affect the clone

criteria.text: Clone succeeds from a paused session
Source session remains paused (clone does not resume it)

requirements.agentNotes: A paused session still has its full directory, so cloning is perfectly valid.

requirements.title: Clone from completed session rejected

ears.if: If a clone is requested from a session with status 'completed'
ears.shall: then the system shall reject the clone with an error suggesting `sisyphus start` or `sisyphus continue` instead

criteria.text: Error message states that completed sessions cannot be cloned
Error suggests alternatives: `sisyphus start` for a new session or `sisyphus continue` to resume

requirements.agentNotes: Completed sessions are done. Cloning a completed session is ambiguous — use `continue` to resume or `start` for a fresh session.

requirements.title: Empty context directory

ears.when: When a clone is requested from a session whose context/ directory is empty or contains only CLAUDE.md
ears.shall: the system shall proceed with the clone normally — an empty context is valid

criteria.text: Clone succeeds even with no context files beyond CLAUDE.md
The programmatic orientation context still references inherited history generically

requirements.agentNotes: Early-stage sessions may not have accumulated context yet. The clone still has cycle history, prompts, and reports even without context files.

requirements.title: Multiple clones from same source

ears.when: When multiple clone requests are made from the same source session
ears.shall: the system shall create independent clones each time — each clone gets its own ID and is unrelated to other clones

criteria.text: Each clone gets a unique session ID
Clones are unaware of each other
Source session is unmodified by any number of clones
Each clone's files have the source session ID replaced with its own unique ID

requirements.agentNotes: No artificial limit on clones. Each is an independent session.

requirements.title: Source session not found

ears.if: If the session ID from the environment does not match any registered session
ears.shall: then the system shall return an error indicating the source session was not found

criteria.text: Error message includes the session ID that was not found
Exits with code 1

requirements.agentNotes: Standard error handling.

requirements.title: History events on clone

ears.shall: the system shall emit a `session-cloned` history event on the source session and a `cloned-from` history event on the clone session

criteria.text: Source session gets a `session-cloned` event with the clone's session ID and goal
Clone session gets a `cloned-from` event with the source session ID
Both events are informational only — no behavioral side effects

requirements.agentNotes: Bidirectional audit trail — both sessions know about the clone relationship in their history, but no runtime behavior depends on it.
